title: camissonia brevipes subsp. arizonica
intro: Camissonia brevipes subsp. arizonica, also known as Arizona desert marigold or Arizona suncup, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the evening primrose family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, salves, or tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
